trails.description: Located beyond Port Jackson campsite, this stunning coastal walk leaves the Muriwai Hilltop car park and heads along a benched farm track along the ridge  towards Wharekaiatua Pa.

Matarangi Bluff Track
trails.description: This popular short walk connects Matarangi and Rings Beach. It provides spectacular views over Matarangi, Kūaotunu and the Coromandel Peninsula.

trails.name: Rings Beach Loop Track
trails.description: Enjoy the spectacular views as you wind through the regenerating forest and wetlands between Matarangi and Rings Beach.

trails.name: Burrill Route
trails.description: Burrill Route was formed in the 1970s by miners prospecting for copper.

trails.name: Coromandel Walkway and mountain bike track
trails.description: Walk or mountain bike between Fletcher Bay and Stony Bay.

trails.name: Fantail Bay Track
trails.description: A tramping track leaves from the Fantail Bay campsite and leads to the bushline where you’ll find outstanding views.

trails.description: Discover a wide array of beautiful bush and mountain views.

trails.name: Harataonga area tracks
trails.description: There are a number of tramping tracks in the Harataonga area, which offer great views of the island and surrounding coastline.

trails.name: Kaipawa Trig Track
trails.description: Walk through regenerating forest to a trig.

trails.name: Kaitoke Hot Springs Track to Mt Hobson (Hirakimata)
trails.description: Kaitoke Hot Springs Track is a popular easy walk following the edge of the Kaitoke Swamp to sulphurous hot springs. Continue on the Peach Tree Track to Mt Hobson (Hirakimata).

trails.name: Kauri Block Track
trails.description: This walk follows a ridge through regenerating bush and native plantings beneath the Te Kauri Pā. All visitors to this site are asked to be respectful to these sites of cultural significance.

trails.name: Matamataharakeke Track
trails.description: Part of the original Waikawau to Kennedy Bay route used by gold miners and loggers. It goes up the main ridge past a series of lookouts giving views over bush covered hills to coast and islands.

trails.name: Opera Point Walk
trails.description: Opera Point on the Coromandel Peninsula offers a walk on an old tramway line to a pleasant beach.

trails.name: Port FitzRoy: Cooper's Castle Track
trails.description: Walk to a lookout with extensive views.

trails.name: Port FitzRoy: Kaiaraara Hut side tracks
trails.description: These tracks all start from Kaiaraara Hut, a short distance from the end of Kaiaraara Bay Road, near Port FitzRoy.

trails.name: Stony Batter Historic Reserve walks
trails.description: There are short walks starting from the Stony Batter Historic Reserve.

trails.name: Tramline Track
trails.description: The Tramline Track system traverses most of Great Barrier island at its widest point.

trails.name: Waitaia Track
trails.description: Once extensively logged and mined, a regenerating coastal forest now greets visitors as they walk along the Waitaia Track.

trails.name: Whangaparapara: Old Mill Track
trails.description: Discover the site of the Kauri Timber Company sawmill and an historic steam traction engine.

trails.name: Whangaparapara: Te Ahumata Track
trails.description: Discover interesting flora and panoramic views at the top of Te Ahumata (398 m).

trails.name: Whangaparapara: Tramline and Withey Tracks
trails.description: Combine these tracks with Forest Road for a circuit walk from Whangaparapara.

trails.name: Whitianga Rock Walk
trails.description: A short walking track from Ferry Landing Wharf leads into Whitianga Rock Scenic and Historic Reserve where you can visit the remains of a pa site.

trails.name: Windy Canyon and Palmer's Track to Mt Hobson (Hirakimata)
trails.description: Windy Canyon is a popular walk on Great Barrier Island, offering spectacular views. You can then continue on Palmers Track to the summit of Mt Hobson (Hirakimata).

trails.name: Kaiaraara Track to Mt Hobson (Hirakimata)
trails.description: Walk to the remains of the old kauri dam, and up to the summit of Mt Hobson (Hirakimata) for a 360-degree panorama.

trails.name: Port FitzRoy: Old Lady Track
trails.description: Walk to a lookout rock with impressive views over Port FitzRoy harbour.

trails.name: Burrill Route
trails.description: Burrill Route was formed in the 1970s by miners prospecting for copper.

trails.name: Forest Road (Port FitzRoy   Whangaparapara)
trails.description: Forest Road is a popular trek for both trampers and mountain bikers, providing a wide array of beautiful bush and mountain views.

trails.name: Harataonga area tracks
trails.description:
There are a number of tramping tracks in the Harataonga area, which offer great views of the island and surrounding coastline. 

Harataonga Coastal Walkway
Time: 4 - 5 hr one way

This easy walk does not have the steep grades of many of the island's other tracks. It winds through regenerating forest and was once the road linking the island's south and north. From Harataonga campsite, the track heads west across a stream before following a coastline of magnificant views. The track ends at Aotea Road at the south end of Okiwi Basin.

The Harataonga Coastal Walkway is not a mountain bike route.

 Harataonga Loop Track
Time: 30 min

This track branches to the right, close to the Harataonga end of the Coastal Walkway. The track loops back to the campground passing close to a pa site. Enjoy sea and coastal views.

trails.name: Kaitoke Hot Springs Track to Mt Hobson (Hirakimata)
trails.description:
Kaitoke Hot Springs Track is a popular easy walk following the edge of the Kaitoke Swamp to sulphurous hot springs. Continue on the Peach Tree Track to Mt Hobson (Hirakimata).

Kaitoke Hot Springs Track
Time: 1 hr one way

Starting from Whangaparapara Road, the journey to the top begins flat and easy - very friendly for push-chairs. The path follows an ancient shoreline traversing the Kaitoke wetlands and regenerating kānuka forest. You may hear the call of a fernbird or spotless crake. Orchids and sun dews flower close to the track. Forty-five minutes later, the track reaches the hot pools, dammed at a fork in Kaitoke Creek and surrounded by the delicate umbrella fern

Peach Tree Track to Mt Hobson (Hirakimata)
Time: 5 hr one way

From the hot pools, after a brief, steep climb and descent, the track joins Tramline Track with reminders of loggers’ toil and journeys of 80 years ago. Soon after, Peach Tree Track appears on the left and the climb to the summit begins through regenerating forest.

Thirty to forty-five minutes from the summit, the track passes Mt Heale and Mt Heale Hut. Wake up to wide views from the northwest through to the south.

trails.name: Port FitzRoy: Cooper's Castle Track
trails.description: Walk to a lookout with extensive views.

trails.name: Port FitzRoy: Kaiaraara Hut side tracks

trails.name: Port FitzRoy: Warren's and Bridle Tracks
trails.description: Warren's Track is a gently graded climb to a waterfall and good swimming spots.

trails.name: Stony Batter Historic Reserve walks

trails.name: Tramline Track
trails.description: The Tramline Track system traverses most of Great Barrier island at its widest point.

trails.name: Whangaparapara: Te Ahumata Track
trails.description: Discover interesting flora and panoramic views at the top of Te Ahumata (398 m).

trails.name: Whangaparapara: Tramline, Pack & Withey Tracks
trails.description:
Combine these tracks with Forest Road for a circuit walk from Whangaparapara. 

Tramline Track
Time: 6 hr one way

The Tramline Track, which extends about 8 km from Whangaparapara to Aotea Road, traverses most of the island at its widest point. The Whangaparapara end of the track progresses over several bridges up to Forest Road (1 hour).

Withey's Track
Time: 1 hr 30 min one way

The track is named after Bill Withey, a steam hauler operator for the Kauri Timber Company. Withey’s Track links to Forest Road, Tramline Track forming a round hike from Whangaparapara. Branching off Tramline Track, it climbs steadily before dropping to Wairahi Stream, crossing three times (no bridges) before linking with Forest Road. Beautiful bush and stream views.

The Pack Track is permanently closed.

trails.name: Windy Canyon and Palmer's Track to Mt Hobson (Hirakimata)
trails.description: Windy Canyon is a popular walk on Great Barrier Island, offering spectacular views. You can then continue on Palmers Track to the summit of Mt Hobson (Hirakimata).
